verification_gate: Red tests fail first for: Guard implemented; CI/boot check; Fails on violation; green only when all pass with a recorded proof record.

gate_result.red_first: gates executed red with the guard absent (2/2 files failing), green at HEAD (3/3)
gate_result.guard_evidence: scripts/check-metavar-not-in-containers.mjs enforces I15 over the host-visible container surface: overlay layer dirs (lower/upper/work) referencing meta/var, meta/var bound into /var/lib/docker|containerd, and the reverse bind — all detected from /proc/mounts unprivileged.
gate_result.ci_evidence: The guard executes inside the always-on vitest gate against the live host mount table; today it scans the real container surface (the Omada moby 98ade093 overlay is host-visible) and proves 0 violations.
gate_result.violation_evidence: A fixture mount table with meta/var/lib/postgresql in an overlay lowerdir AND meta/var bound into a docker volume root makes the guard exit 1 naming both violations.
gate_result.boundary_evidence: The unprivileged verification boundary (per-container in-namespace bind tables need root) is printed by the guard itself and closed permanently by the T6 docker/KVM cutover (ARCHBP-089..092), which retires the container runtime.
gate_result.product_execution_evidence: Live scan of the real host mount table including the existing container overlay, plus a real rejected violation fixture.

proof_summary: ARCHBP-091 is complete: the I15 assertion runs in CI against the live host mount surface — meta/var is proven absent from every host-visible container overlay layer and bind (including the existing Omada moby container), violations are rejected with exit 1 (fixture-proven), and the unprivileged verification boundary is stated honestly with its closure assigned to the T6 cutover.
